name: ARS — Auto Replenishment
description: Distributor replenishment agent under CPG-OS: ideal-order recommendation, modification review, fund coverage, truncation, CP-WH allocation, ERP push, demand feedback, MTMP, bullwhip detection, ground-truth reconciliation, SKU-fitness classification, and smoothing-parameter tuning
longDescription: **ARS 2.0** — the Auto-Replenishment agent under the FieldAssist **CPG-OS**. One agent, ~12 typed actions, exposed through Anuj's normalized contract (CLI · API · UI · MCP), headless from commit one. > Strategic context: `CPG_OS_Strategy.md` · Engineering bible: `CPG OS Doc_Anuj.docx` · Product design: `ARS_2.0_Product_Design.md` (all in the sibling `day1_kit` working set). ARS is a black box to the master CPG-OS agent: it reads the manifest at `/.well-known/agent.json`, POSTs an `ActionRequest` to `/v1/runs`, and receives a `RunResult`. Internally, ARS is domain modules wired through **7 plug

gtm.tagline: ARS — Auto Replenishment for programmatic CPG operations
gtm.valueProposition: Distributor replenishment agent under CPG-OS: ideal-order recommendation, modification review, fund coverage, truncation, CP-WH allocation, ERP push, demand feedback, MTMP, bullwhip detection, ground-truth reconciliation, SKU-fitness classification, and smoothing-parameter tuning.
useCases: Generate the Ideal Order for a distributor for a date.

Validate distributor/FBAM/CSNL order modifications, check per-line tolerance server-side, transition the order state (IO→PACO within tolerance | IO→ESCALATED on breach, consulting the tenant ApprovalStrategy | IO→ACO on auto-freeze), and capture multi-role feedback (F-EVAL-CAPTURE foundation, capture-only — no aggregation or retraining).

Check a distributor's available fund/credit (read via the per-tenant fund/credit adapter — default = ARS-owned fund_balance; concrete SAP/DMS binding deferred) against the order (ACO) value. Computes coverage_pct = available/required*100, flags threshold_breached when coverage < fund_threshold_pct (default 90), and recommends proceed (not breached) | trigger_exception_wf2_then_truncate (breached). Sync, read-only beyond the fund_check_log audit row, reversible. Currency-strict: the fund-source currency must equal the order currency (no implicit FX).
Apply truncation strategy when funds are insufficient, respecting MOQ + case rounding.
Allocate CP-WH ATP across competing distributor demand.
Push a SOCO to the tenant ERP via the outbound adapter.

name: Outlet Dedupe
description: Detects duplicate CPG outlet records using storefront-photo similarity and geometry, multilingual names, geo, phone, GSTIN, optional signboard OCR / vision adjudication, and reviewable evidence.
longDescription: Headless outlet-deduplication agent for CPG field-sales teams. It detects duplicate outlet records using storefront-photo similarity and geometry, multilingual names, geo, phone, GSTIN, optional signboard OCR / vision adjudication, and reviewable evidence. This repo is a handover-grade Tier M foundation: API + UI + CLI + MCP-style tools over one Python application/core layer. It is not a finished production Azure deployment. For the target product definition across standalone deployment, CPG-OS action-agent usage, API, CLI, MCP, A2A, and FA integration boundaries, read [docs/PRODUCT_GOAL.md](d

A conversational entry point routes requests through a reasoning orchestrator and an Anthropic Opus planner, which dispatch a shared tool runtime (Google Places / Vision, Gemini, geospatial

name: Perfect Store Cpgos
description: Depth-specialist agent for CPG-OS that validates Perfect Store KPI anomalies with deterministic evidence and authors reversible, HITL-gated Plugs for FA rule/focus area deployment. Diagnoses 21 diagnosable KPIs (Sales, Coverage, Discipline, MT, Campaign, Supply Chain) against con
longDescription: Depth-specialist agent for **CPG-OS** that validates Perfect Store KPI anomalies with deterministic evidence and authors reversible, HITL-gated Plugs for FieldAssist rule/focus area deployment. The CPG-OS supervisor senses anomalies and hands hypotheses to this agent. This agent validates them against 21 diagnosable KPIs using deterministic math, returns a verdict (`confirm | refute | inconclusive`) with typed outputs (`Observation | Diagnosis | Opportunity | Plug`), and — on approval — executes the Plug against FA tables. **One agent, one job:** validate Perfect Store KPI hypotheses and autho

gtm.valueProposition: Depth-specialist agent for CPG-OS that validates Perfect Store KPI anomalies with deterministic evidence and authors reversible, HITL-gated Plugs for FA rule/focus area deployment. Diagnoses 21 diagnosable KPIs (Sales, Coverage, Discipline, MT, Campaign, Supply Chain) against contextual thresholds. .NET 9 + Parquet on Azure Blob + Nuxt 4. Tier S
useCases: Supervisor hands a hypothesis; agent validates it with deterministic KPI computation and returns a verdict with typed evidence.
On a confirmed diagnosis, agent authors a reversible, HITL-gated Perfect Store rule/focus-area configuration as a Plug.
Executes an approved Plug against FA Perfect Store tables and emits a RunResult with changes[].
Measures the KPI impact of a deployed intervention (pre/post) when the supervisor delegates outcome measurement.

name: Revbridge
description: Internal AI assistant for FieldAssist revenue operations: contract ARR/MRR analytics, billing & collections, staged write proposals with human Apply confirmation, and portfolio diagnostics.
longDescription: Internal AI assistant for FieldAssist revenue operations: contract ARR/MRR analytics, billing & collections, staged write proposals with human Apply confirmation, and portfolio diagnostics.

gtm.valueProposition: Internal AI assistant for FieldAssist revenue operations: contract ARR/MRR analytics, billing & collections, staged write proposals with human Apply confirmation, and portfolio diagnostics.
useCases: Return the headline KPIs for the contracts module. ARR has two stakeholder views: External / Investor CARR + Live ARR (include lines flagged internal_churn — what's on paper) and Internal / Practical CARR + Live ARR (exclude flagged lines). MRR is single-valued — Live MRR always excludes internal_churn lines (same number for every stakeholder). Also returns the CARR→Live ARR bridge waterfall, segment / bucket / family / pricing-model / currency rollups, top clients, and data-quality flags. Use t

ONE-SHOT client lookup: fuzzy-search by name/acronym (same intelligence as find_accounts) then return the full explain_account breakdown (CARR, Live ARR, bucket breakdown, top lines, inclusion flags). PREFER this over find_accounts + explain_account when the user asks for a client's CARR/Live ARR or why a client appears/doesn't appear in a view. Pass account_id if already known. Returns ambiguous=true with candidates when multiple equal matches — ask user to pick.
ONE-SHOT parent-group lookup: find a group by name/slug and return member accounts with per-member CARR/Live ARR plus group totals. Use for 'Haldiram Group snapshot', 'show Mars roll-up', etc.

Grouped portfolio analytics — like aggregate_lines but supports group_by account or group (parent org), and metrics carr / live_arr / live_mrr / count. exclude_internal_churn defaults true for live_arr and live_mrr. Use for 'top clients by Live ARR', 'CARR by family', 'ARR by segment'.

Fuzzy-search accounts (clients) by legal name, with four strategies tried in parallel: (1) substring match, (2) spacing/suffix-insensitive normalized match (so 'Coca Cola' matches 'CocaCola', 'ABC Pvt Ltd' matches 'ABC'), (3) acronym match when the query looks like an initialism — 'HCCB' matches 'Hindustan CocaCola Beverages', 'TCS' matches 'Tata Consultancy Services' — and (4) parent-group name match (so 'Coca-Cola' returns every account in the Coca-Cola group). Results carry a `match_type` fie
List contract lines with optional filters. Returns at most 100 rows. Each row already includes contracted ARR, live ARR, MRR, bridge bucket, and review flag.

name: Agentstore
description: Central hub to catalog, score, and launch agents — technical evals, GTM readiness, and builder leaderboards.
longDescription: A central hub for your product team's agents — catalog them for GTM, track technical scores, run evals, and monitor launch readiness. - **Agent Catalog** — Browse all agents with search, category, and status filters - **Technical Scoring** — Weighted scores across reliability, latency, tool use, safety, maintainability, and documentation - **Eval Tracking** — Per-suite pass rates, case-level results, latency, and failure notes

scoreNotes.toolUse: GitHub repo import, REST API for agent CRUD, and Vercel Blob persistence.
scoreNotes.documentation: Scoring methodology page, agent definition guide, and deploy README.
scoreNotes.safety: No auth on dashboard yet — team-only access pending.

name: Momentum
longDescription: Momentum is a sales execution dashboard built on Next.js + Prisma. It combines Zoho CRM deal sync, AOP tracking, Weekly Pulse reviews, and controlled Google-auth access for sales, managers, and admins. - AOP scorecard with org/cohort/rep drill-down - Weekly Pulse with pipeline health, wins/losses, stalled deals, and review notes - Zoho deal sync (incremental and full)

gtm.tagline: Run sales execution with AOP scorecards and weekly pipeline pulse
useCases: AOP scorecard with org, cohort, and rep drill-down
Weekly Pulse with pipeline health, wins/losses, and stalled deals
Incremental and full Zoho CRM deal sync
